behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels." Slot B: model-output boundary (real STT model diarization quality). Slot C: if lowered to System with fixed/mock transcripts, tests can miss real diarization failures (speaker swaps/missing labels) that break attribution. Final label: Agentic because Slot B is real model output and Slot C is hidden by non-model fixtures. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text focuses on STT label preservation, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real STT on representative multi-speaker meeting audio and assert transcript includes correct per-utterance speaker labels that map to attendees used by summarization input.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments ... into the summary input structure." Slot B: local parsing/transform logic boundary. Slot C: if lowered below Unit, malformed extraction (missing assignee or task text) is not directly validated at all. Final label: Unit because Slot B is single-function/object transformation and Slot C is caught there earliest. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration/Agentic because behavior text targets deterministic parsing into structure, not OS/network/model-output correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixtures with explicit assignment lines and assert exact structured fields (assignee, task text) including edge cases like punctuation and multiple assignments in one turn.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task." Slot B: model-output boundary (LLM must consistently include required attendee-linked items). Slot C: if lowered to Integration with stubbed summaries, omission failures for some attendees are hidden. Final label: Agentic because Slot B depends on real LLM judgment and Slot C is exactly what stubs mask. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text does not require validating a multi-agentic chain, only final summary content quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use real summarization model on transcripts with known attendee-task assignments; assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one action item in output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields." Slot B: local routing/schema-mapping boundary. Slot C: if lowered below Unit, field-routing regressions (decisions written to narrative) are untested. Final label: Unit because Slot B is deterministic mapping logic and Slot C is directly detectable without external infra. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because phrase "identified decisions" indicates decision identification is already done; this behavior is routing, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Given pre-identified decision objects, assert mapper writes them only to `decisions` schema keys and never to narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Key decisions ... appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative." Slot B: model-output boundary (LLM extraction and placement quality). Slot C: if lowered to Integration with canned model output, real misses where the model narrates decisions without structuring them are hidden. Final label: Agentic because Slot B requires real model behavior and Slot C is a real content-quality failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run real summarization on transcripts containing explicit decision statements; assert those decisions are present in `decisions` and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary." Slot B: multi-step agentic boundary (repeated STT/LLM steps plus merge across segments). Slot C: if lowered to Agentic (single call) or System with stubs, cross-segment ordering/context-loss/merge incoherence failures are missed. Final label: Workflow because Slot B is explicitly sequential multi-step agentic processing and Slot C is cross-step failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real long-audio path end-to-end over >60 min recording; verify segment sequence handling, merged output structure, and cross-segment coherence (no dropped/duplicated major items).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) ... for a real meeting recording." Slot B: multi-step agentic + real-infra boundary across full pipeline. Slot C: if lowered to System or Agentic in isolation, orchestration breaks between stages (handoff/state/format mismatches requiring manual fixes) can be missed. Final label: Workflow because Slot B spans sequential real model stages and Slot C is an end-to-end handoff failure.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full production-like run on a real meeting file from upload to delivered structured summary; assert unattended completion and valid final schema with action items/decisions/attributions populated.
